content:
{
  "title": "Bias-Reflective Prompting: Dynamic Counterfactual Prompts to Mitigate Stereotypes in Language Models",
  "problem": "Large language models often reproduce or amplify social biases and stereotypes present in their training data, leading to problematic or unfair outputs. Existing static prompting strategies struggle to adaptively address context-specific bias manifestations.",
  "motivation": "Reducing stereotype propagation in LLMs is critical for equitable and responsible AI deployment, especially as these models are increasingly used in sensitive decision-making and information dissemination. Current methods largely rely on post-hoc filtering or fine-tuning, which can be resource-intensive and inflexible to real-time user inputs.",
  "method": "We propose 'Bias-Reflective Prompting,' a dynamic prompting technique that automatically generates counterfactual role reversals within user prompts. For each input containing a potentially bias-prone entity (e.g., gender, ethnicity, occupation), the system creates parallel prompts with swapped or neutralized demographic attributes. The model's responses are then compared in real-time; if disparities indicative of bias or stereotype reinforcement are detected, the final output is revised using an ensemble or consensus mechanism to favor less biased completions. This approach requires no model fine-tuning and operates as a prompt-based wrapper.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate Bias-Reflective Prompting on benchmark datasets such as StereoSet and CrowS-Pairs, as well as real-world user queries. Baselines will include vanilla prompting, adversarial prompting, and instruction-based debiasing. Metrics will cover stereotype scores, bias amplification measures, and human-rated fairness/fluency. Ablation studies will assess the effect of different counterfactual generation strategies."
}
